teks: FROM A SINGLE IDEA TO IMAGE, STORY, AND VIDEO
prompt:
Create a premium cinematic 16:9 opening image for an educational 3D animated presentation. Use the uploaded Little Robot character sheet and School Garden environment sheet as mandatory references. Preserve the robot's exact rounded pearl-white helmet, dark navy face screen, cyan oval eyes, cyan chest core, violet accents, compact child-friendly proportions, hands, feet, and materials. Preserve the exact garden geography, cream two-story school, blue-framed windows, central tree, circular wooden bench, pale stone paths, metal fence, arched entrance, planting beds, irrigation pipes, gardening-tool board, flowers, vegetables, and bright-morning atmosphere.

Show a wide hero view from just outside the garden entrance. Little Robot stands beside the open arched gate and looks into the garden with a curious, welcoming pose, one hand gently presenting the pathway. Build the title into a real architectural object: attach a wide premium teak signboard securely across the garden entrance structure. The board must have believable wood grain, thickness, mounting brackets, contact shadows, and weather-resistant finish. Carve the exact text "FROM A SINGLE IDEA TO IMAGE, STORY, AND VIDEO" deeply into the teak, using clean uppercase lettering filled with subtle cyan and warm-gold resin. The lettering must follow the board perspective, catch the same sunlight, and look physically engraved rather than digitally added. Add a small trail of tangible creative objects along the path—a closed sketchbook, a printed image card, a bound storybook, and a compact video camera—arranged naturally as visual storytelling props without labels.

Composition: cinematic wide establishing shot, child-height viewpoint, 28–35 mm lens feeling, strong depth, clear foreground gate, robot in the middle ground, school and garden behind, warm sunlight from upper right, soft blue ambient light, polished family-friendly 3D animation, clean visual hierarchy, safe margins, high resolution.

NEGATIVE REQUIREMENTS: no floating text, no subtitles, no lower thirds, no title overlay, no holographic words, no speech bubbles, no captions, no UI, no watermark, no logo, no extra text, no misspelled letters, no detached sign, no flat pasted typography, no redesigned robot, no additional robots, no children or adults, no moved garden landmarks, no weapons, no extra limbs, no deformed hands, no cropped title board, no night scene, no photorealistic live-action style.

teks: WHAT WILL WE DO? | IDEA | IMAGE | STORY | VIDEO
prompt:
Create a polished cinematic 16:9 educational 3D animation image that explains a four-step creative journey inside the established School Garden. Use the uploaded Little Robot character sheet and School Garden environment sheet as mandatory references. Keep the Little Robot and every major garden landmark exactly consistent with those references.

Stage the camera in a medium-wide view looking down the pale stone pathway from the entrance toward the central tree. Little Robot is walking along the path with an enthusiastic but natural learning pose, carrying a small closed sketchbook under one arm. Integrate the slide text into permanent physical garden wayfinding: place one low glazed-ceramic header tile at the beginning of the path with the exact engraved text "WHAT WILL WE DO?". Then embed four separate square ceramic stepping-stones into the existing path, each with one exact inlaid word in this sequence: "IDEA", "IMAGE", "STORY", "VIDEO". Use distinct but harmonious cyan, violet, teal, and warm-gold glazed inlays. The letters must be recessed into the tiles, slightly worn at the edges, aligned to the path perspective, and affected by sunlight and contact shadows. Connect the four tiles using a thin physical brass line embedded into the stone path, not a floating arrow.

Add tangible storytelling props near each step without clutter: a pencil and seed-shaped sketch token near IDEA, a small printed picture card near IMAGE, a bound miniature storybook near STORY, and a compact camera on a garden bench near VIDEO. All objects must rest on real surfaces with correct scale and shadows. The garden remains bright, colorful, safe, and cheerful.

Composition: readable left-to-right or foreground-to-background progression, 35 mm cinematic lens, gentle leading lines, robot as the emotional anchor, central tree and school building recognizable, warm bright-morning lighting, subtle depth of field, clean high-end animated feature style.

NEGATIVE REQUIREMENTS: no floating words, no subtitle strip, no infographic overlay, no hologram, no speech bubble, no UI cards, no arrows floating in the air, no text in the sky, no labels detached from objects, no random letters, no extra text, no misspelling, no duplicated robot, no changed robot anatomy, no moved architecture, no crowded props, no children, no watermark, no logo, no cropped pathway text, no extreme top-down angle that makes the robot unreadable.

teks: AI IS A TOOL | THE CHILD DIRECTS THE IDEA
prompt:
Create a premium 16:9 cinematic 3D animated image expressing that AI is a helpful tool while creative direction remains human-led. Use the uploaded Little Robot character sheet and School Garden environment sheet as mandatory references. Reproduce the exact robot identity and the exact gardening-tool area from the school garden.

Place Little Robot at the established wooden gardening workbench beside the real tool board. The robot is using ordinary garden tools to repair a small planter while an open sketchbook on the bench shows a simple hand-drawn plan of the planter. Its posture should communicate assistance, focus, patience, and service—not authority. Use tangible visual metaphors: a rounded wrench, pencil, ruler, seed packets, and watering can are organized neatly as equally useful tools. No tool floats.

Integrate the exact text into one believable object from the template: fasten a brushed stainless-steel teaching plaque to the wooden tool board using four visible rounded bolts. Laser-engrave the exact first line "AI IS A TOOL" in large uppercase letters. Directly beneath it, engrave the exact smaller line "THE CHILD DIRECTS THE IDEA". The engraving must cut into the metal surface, show subtle anisotropic reflections, follow the plaque perspective, and share the scene lighting. Add a small embossed pencil-and-wrench symbol physically stamped into the plaque, with no additional wording. The plaque must feel like permanent school-garden signage, not a presentation overlay.

Composition: medium-wide shot, robot and workbench centered, school and central tree softly visible behind, 40 mm cinematic lens, warm morning light, cyan rim reflection from the robot, accurate contact shadows, clean organized environment, sophisticated child-friendly 3D animation style.

NEGATIVE REQUIREMENTS: no floating typography, no subtitle, no lower third, no title overlay, no holographic interface, no digital speech bubble, no glowing words in the air, no extra text, no spelling errors, no detached metal plaque, no paper label pasted over the scene, no robot acting as a human replacement, no second robot, no people, no altered robot design, no dangerous tools, no sparks, no weapons, no clutter, no moved garden landmarks, no logos, no watermark.

teks: A LITTLE ROBOT SAVES THE SCHOOL GARDEN
prompt:
Create a cinematic 16:9 key art image for the mini project "A Little Robot Saves the School Garden". Use the uploaded Little Robot character sheet and School Garden environment sheet as strict visual references. Maintain the robot's exact face screen, eyes, white shell, navy joints, cyan core, violet accents, body proportions, hands, and feet. Maintain the exact school building, entrance gate, central tree, circular bench, raised beds, paths, fence, pipes, and tool board.

Show one coherent story moment with cause, action, and hope visible in a single environment. Little Robot kneels safely near a loosened irrigation connector beside one raised vegetable bed. One hand stabilizes the pipe and the other holds a small rounded wrench. A few plants nearest the damaged pipe are wilted, while healthy flowers farther away show what the garden can become again. A gentle test droplet appears at the repaired connector. The robot's cyan eyes communicate focused determination.

Integrate the exact title into the garden entrance: mount a substantial carved teak project sign on the inside of the arched gate. Deeply engrave "A LITTLE ROBOT SAVES THE SCHOOL GARDEN" across two balanced lines. Fill the carved letters with durable warm-white enamel and thin cyan accents. The wooden sign must have realistic thickness, grain, bolts, bevels, sunlight, and contact shadow. It must belong to the location like a named school project plaque, never like a movie title pasted over the image.

Composition: wide three-quarter garden view with the title sign readable in the foreground, robot repair action in the middle ground, school and central tree behind, 28–35 mm lens feeling, bright morning with slightly hopeful cinematic contrast, polished family-friendly 3D animation, readable silhouettes, detailed but uncluttered.

NEGATIVE REQUIREMENTS: no floating title, no subtitle, no poster overlay, no text in the sky, no hologram, no speech bubbles, no extra words, no misspelling, no redesigned garden, no different school, no extra robots, no children, no dangerous machinery, no electrical sparks, no severe flooding, no dead garden, no weapon-like tools, no extra limbs or fingers, no deformed robot, no watermark, no logo, no cropped title sign.

Create a polished 16:9 cinematic educational image showing a three-scene story structure as a real physical installation inside the established School Garden. Use the uploaded Little Robot character sheet and School Garden environment sheet as mandatory references. Keep the exact robot and environment identity consistent.

Build a permanent three-panel ceramic mosaic display into the low garden wall beside the main path. The display must be made from thick glazed tiles set into a real masonry frame with grout, bevels, sunlight, and contact shadows. Across the top stone lintel, carve the exact text "3-SCENE STORY STRUCTURE". Beneath it, create three equal physical mosaic panels from left to right. Panel one depicts Little Robot discovering wilted plants and has the exact word "DISCOVERY" fired into its ceramic lower border. Panel two depicts the same robot repairing the same irrigation pipe and has the exact word "ACTION" fired into its lower border. Panel three depicts the restored green garden with sprinklers and has the exact word "RESOLUTION" fired into its lower border. The labels must be part of the tile glaze, not captions placed on top.

Place the real Little Robot in front of the installation, gently pointing from the first panel toward the third with an open teaching gesture. The actual garden behind the display should subtly echo the same story location, with the affected bed and irrigation area recognizable. Use only the three required labels and header text.

Composition: medium-wide view at child eye level, display filling most of the frame while still grounded in the garden, 35–40 mm lens, clear left-to-right progression, warm bright-morning light, premium stylized 3D animation, realistic ceramic materials, crisp readable lettering, soft shadows, no clutter.

NEGATIVE REQUIREMENTS: no floating labels, no subtitle bar, no UI cards, no digital infographic, no arrow overlays, no holograms, no speech bubbles, no extra scene panels, no extra text, no misspelling, no repeated or inconsistent robot designs inside the mosaics, no detached frames, no cropped labels, no rearranged school garden, no people, no watermark, no logo, no illegible microtext, no photorealistic live action.

teks: FROM IDEA TO PROMPT | CHARACTER + SETTING + MOOD = CLEAR PROMPT
prompt:
Create a premium 16:9 cinematic 3D animated image explaining how an idea becomes a clear AI prompt. Use the uploaded Little Robot character sheet and School Garden environment sheet as strict references. Preserve the exact robot model and the recognizable garden work area.

Set the scene at a real wooden planning desk placed naturally beside the central circular bench. Little Robot sits or stands at the desk, carefully arranging three thick physical reference cards into slots on a wooden prompt-building board. The first card contains a small printed portrait of the same robot and the exact embossed word "CHARACTER". The second contains a printed view of the same school garden and the exact embossed word "SETTING". The third contains a bright-morning color swatch and the exact embossed word "MOOD". The cards must be tangible printed objects with paper fibers, rounded corners, thickness, slot shadows, and correct perspective.

On the desk, place an open premium notebook. Deboss the exact header "FROM IDEA TO PROMPT" into its leather cover strip. On the visible cream page, print the exact formula "CHARACTER + SETTING + MOOD = CLEAR PROMPT" using dark navy ink with small cyan separators. The formula must follow the page curvature and lighting and appear as real ink on paper. The robot holds a pencil or stylus against the page, showing an intentional creative process. No words may hover above the cards or notebook.

Composition: medium-wide over-the-shoulder three-quarter view, robot and prompt board readable, garden beds and school softly visible, 40 mm lens, warm morning light, subtle depth of field, polished family-friendly 3D animation, clean educational storytelling, realistic paper, leather, and wood materials.

NEGATIVE REQUIREMENTS: no floating text, no subtitle, no UI overlay, no black prompt box floating in space, no holographic words, no browser window, no speech bubbles, no extra text, no misspelling, no detached cards, no illegible formula, no changed robot or garden, no second robot, no people, no random office environment, no watermark, no logo, no cropped notebook, no deformed hands, no duplicated props.

Create a cinematic 16:9 educational 3D animation image showing how finished scene images become a storyboard. Use the uploaded Little Robot character sheet and School Garden environment sheet as mandatory references. Preserve the exact robot and garden production design.

Place a large physical storyboard board on the established wooden gardening workbench. The board must be a real framed object made from pale wood and cork, supported by sturdy feet, with believable thickness, pins, edge shadows, and sunlight. Pin twelve small printed frames to the board in a clean 4-by-3 grid. Every frame should depict a coherent progression in the same garden: establishing shot, robot entering, discovering wilted plants, repairing the pipe, water returning, plants recovering, garden transformation, robot satisfied, robot exiting, and final wide garden shot. Keep the robot identity and garden geography consistent across all printed frames. Use simple hand-drawn colored arrows printed or drawn directly onto the individual paper frames to indicate body movement, camera movement, composition, light, and effects.

Carve the exact header "FROM IMAGES TO STORYBOARD" into the board's upper wooden frame. Attach a small brushed-metal plaque to the bottom frame with the exact engraved text "10-SECOND STORYBOARD". Both texts must be physically attached to the board and follow perspective and lighting. Little Robot stands beside the board holding one paper frame and a red pencil, carefully placing the final frame into position.

Composition: medium-wide view, storyboard board dominant but fully visible, robot beside it, actual garden and school behind, 35 mm lens, bright morning light, premium family-friendly 3D animation, crisp material detail, readable physical header and plaque, organized visual hierarchy.

NEGATIVE REQUIREMENTS: no floating storyboard, no subtitle, no digital UI overlay, no text outside physical wood or metal surfaces, no giant hovering arrows, no holograms, no extra words, no misspelling, no random frame count, no inconsistent robot, no changing school architecture, no detached paper without pins, no illegible title, no people, no watermark, no logo, no cropped storyboard board, no duplicated limbs.

teks: FROM STORYBOARD TO VIDEO | 10-SECOND CINEMATIC SEQUENCE
prompt:
Create a premium cinematic 16:9 3D animated image representing the transition from a physical storyboard to a finished video. Use the uploaded Little Robot character sheet and School Garden environment sheet as strict references. Keep the exact robot identity and the exact garden architecture, paths, beds, tree, bench, irrigation system, and tool area.

Transform one corner of the School Garden into a believable outdoor review station without changing the garden geography. Place a compact real projector on the wooden bench and a freestanding framed projection screen securely positioned beside the central tree. On the screen, show one beautiful still frame of the same Little Robot standing proudly in the restored garden with sprinklers operating. The screen is a physical object with fabric texture, frame thickness, support feet, and cast shadows. Place the completed printed storyboard sheets and a small editing control console on the bench. The console may show a tangible 10-second timeline inside its built-in screen, but it must not resemble a floating interface.

Integrate the exact text into physical hardware. Emboss "FROM STORYBOARD TO VIDEO" into a brushed-metal marquee fixed across the top of the projection-screen frame. Engrave "10-SECOND CINEMATIC SEQUENCE" into a smaller metal plate attached to the projector housing. The lettering must be recessed, correctly spelled, aligned to perspective, and illuminated by the same morning light. Little Robot stands beside the projector with one hand on the real play button and the other presenting the screen.

Composition: medium-wide cinematic view, physical screen and robot clearly readable, garden remains the environment rather than a dark theater, 35–40 mm lens, warm bright-morning light, subtle projector glow that does not overpower daylight, polished child-friendly feature-animation style, clean materials and contact shadows.

NEGATIVE REQUIREMENTS: no floating text, no subtitle, no lower third, no detached video player UI, no hovering timeline, no hologram, no browser chrome, no speech bubbles, no extra words, no misspelling, no black void background, no cinema auditorium, no changed robot, no extra robots or people, no altered garden geography, no cropped screen or metal title, no watermark, no logo, no broken projector geometry, no wires clipping through objects.

teks: WHAT THE CHILD LEARNS | CREATIVITY | STORYTELLING | VISUAL THINKING | PROMPT COMMUNICATION | DECISION MAKING | PRESENTATION
prompt:
Create a polished 16:9 cinematic educational 3D animation image visualizing six learning outcomes inside the established School Garden. Use the uploaded Little Robot character sheet and School Garden environment sheet as mandatory references. Preserve the robot and every recognizable garden landmark exactly.

Center the composition around the large leafy tree and circular wooden bench. Little Robot stands in front of the tree with a proud, encouraging teaching pose. Build the text into permanent garden materials. Engrave the exact header "WHAT THE CHILD LEARNS" into a curved teak band attached securely to the front of the circular bench. Around the tree, embed six premium natural-stone learning markers into the existing path. Each marker must have one exact sandblasted and pigment-filled label: "CREATIVITY", "STORYTELLING", "VISUAL THINKING", "PROMPT COMMUNICATION", "DECISION MAKING", and "PRESENTATION". The letters must be cut into the stone, aligned with each marker's perspective, weather-resistant, and visibly affected by sunlight and shadow.

Pair each marker with one small tangible object resting naturally beside it: a sketchbook for creativity, bound storybook for storytelling, printed image card for visual thinking, prompt notebook for communication, two garden seed choices for decision making, and a small tabletop display easel for presentation. Keep the objects subtle and secondary. Arrange the six markers in a readable semicircle that feels like part of the garden path rather than an infographic.

Composition: wide child-height view, central tree as the anchor, robot and all six markers readable, school building behind, 28–35 mm lens, warm bright-morning light, premium family-friendly 3D animation, clean hierarchy, realistic wood and stone textures, gentle depth of field.

NEGATIVE REQUIREMENTS: no floating skill labels, no subtitle, no UI cards, no holographic icons, no text in the sky, no detached labels, no extra words, no misspelling, no tiny unreadable lettering, no missing skill marker, no duplicate marker, no altered garden path, no additional characters, no redesigned robot, no classroom interior, no watermark, no logo, no cropped header, no random clutter.

teks: COMPLETE 16-SESSION PROGRAM | STORY FOUNDATIONS | STORYBOARD DEVELOPMENT | VISUAL PRODUCTION | VIDEO GENERATION & EDITING | RESPONSIBLE AI PRACTICE | FINAL PRESENTATION & SHOWCASE
prompt:
Create a premium cinematic 16:9 closing image for a complete 16-session creative media program, staged entirely within the established School Garden. Use the uploaded Little Robot character sheet and School Garden environment sheet as strict references. Preserve the exact robot identity and the exact garden geography and architecture.

Show Little Robot at the garden entrance looking down a gently curving path that leads toward a small final showcase easel near the school building. Turn the path into a believable program journey using permanent physical signage. Mount a large carved teak directory board just inside the entrance with the exact engraved header "COMPLETE 16-SESSION PROGRAM". Beneath the header, attach six individual brushed-brass plaques in a clean vertical sequence. Engrave the exact phase names, one per plaque: "STORY FOUNDATIONS", "STORYBOARD DEVELOPMENT", "VISUAL PRODUCTION", "VIDEO GENERATION & EDITING", "RESPONSIBLE AI PRACTICE", and "FINAL PRESENTATION & SHOWCASE". Each plaque must have real thickness, mounting pins, perspective, reflections, and contact shadows. The text must be engraved into the brass, not printed over the image.

Along the real garden path, place six subtle physical milestone objects matching the phases: a bound storybook, framed storyboard sheet, printed character image, compact camera, small shield-shaped responsible-AI emblem with no words, and a final presentation easel. These are real props resting on benches or stands, never floating. Little Robot gestures toward the directory board and the path ahead with an optimistic invitation. The thriving garden communicates growth across the program.

Composition: wide final hero shot, entrance and directory in the foreground, robot in the middle ground, milestone path leading to the school and showcase, 28 mm lens feeling, warm golden bright-morning light, polished family-friendly 3D animation, strong depth, celebratory but professional atmosphere, readable signage, safe margins.

NEGATIVE REQUIREMENTS: no floating curriculum text, no subtitle, no UI timeline, no infographic overlay, no holographic labels, no billboard detached from the environment, no extra words, no misspelling, no missing phase, no repeated phase, no tiny unreadable text, no changed robot, no additional robots or people, no altered school or garden, no cluttered path, no random logos, no watermark, no cropped directory board, no night scene, no graduation caps or unrelated school clichés.
